2_primaryJTBD.decision: What should I do with this player?

3_decisionVsInfoRatio.dominantDecision: No - Intentionally neutral with 4 equal-weight options

4_visualHierarchy.assessment: EXCELLENT - Clear neutral hierarchy per contract

5_kpiQuality.assessment: INTENTIONAL - Zero KPIs by design (neutral hub per contract)
5_kpiQuality.note: Contract explicitly prohibits stats, tests, sessions, summaries

6_languageAndMentalModel.assessment: GOOD - Neutral navigation language as per contract

7_flowAndNavigationCost.assessment: EXCELLENT - All views are 1 click from this hub

gaps: No error handling for player data fetch
No empty state if player not found

9_roleScalability.recommendation: Well-designed neutral hub - scales for all players

10_strategicVerdict.diagnosis: Well-implemented neutral navigation hub following contract

topIssues.issue: No state handling - missing loading/error/empty states
topIssues.impact: Poor experience if player data fails to load

topIssues.issue: Static navigation - no indication of which view has updates
topIssues.impact: Coach may miss new proof or notes (but contract prohibits this)

topIssues.issue: Export button purpose unclear from label
topIssues.impact: Users may not know what gets exported

10_strategicVerdict.structuralFix: Add loading/error states + tooltip for export button

11_crossPageConsistency.note: Uses PageHeader + PageContainer pattern (consistent). Follows strict neutral presentation contract with explicit notes prohibiting summaries, stats, or prioritization. Identical contract adherence to CoachAthleteList.

dataSource.note: No data fetching - receives athleteId + athleteName as props

accessibility.keyboardNav: Good - uses button elements and nav tag

contracts.note: Code includes contract references and strict notes prohibiting stats/summaries/prioritization. Static neutral order enforced.

overallAssessment: EXCELLENT - Clean neutral hub following contract specifications, needs state handling for edge cases
